Explore training

Build technology that solves real problems.

Learn software development, networking, AI, and IoT by working on projects from your first semester.

Explore training

More than classes: training to solve real problems.

The program combines critical thinking, technical practice, and collaboration so students build engineering judgement from early stages.

A professionally oriented program.

Clear academic objective, industry-aligned graduate profile, and continuous connection with real contexts.

Program objective

Train professionals capable of integrating and managing IT and communications to deliver strategic value in organizations.

Graduate profile

Network design, hardware/software integration, and information systems development under international standards.

Professional linkage

Internships, academic visits, and formative events that connect learning with current industry practice.

Explore training →

What will you be able to do?

Develop applications.
Design and manage networks.
Manage data for technical decisions.
Build technological solutions for real problems.

Projects and portfolio.

Learning evidence from labs, applied research, competitions, and academic activities that demonstrate real technical progress.

Institutional portfolio

Activities, projects, and learning experiences documented to show how engineering practice evolves across the program.

Focus areas

Academic tracks in data, interaction, emerging technologies, networks, and intelligent systems.

Labs and practice

Technical spaces and collaborative dynamics to experiment, iterate, and validate solutions in concrete scenarios.

Continuous updates

Specializations reviewed periodically to keep training aligned with current tools and needs.

View projects and portfolio →

Faculty.

Professors with technical experience who guide professional growth in key ICT areas.
